summary: On March 7, 2026, Rubikon Center operators conducted strikes in the Donbas and Krasny Liman direction, destroying 88 Ukrainian Armed Forces personnel and engaging in FPV air defense against fixed-wing UAVs. Rubikon’s air defense units have intercepted over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with 75% belonging to 11 specific models, including 343 Leleka-100 and 132 Furya. Meanwhile, ZALA Lancet operators successfully struck group targets despite electronic warfare interference, demonstrating high efficiency with single-strike tactics. Iran’s new subsonic loitering air defense missile, Missile 358, equipped with thermal imaging homing, has proven effective against Israeli Hermes-900 UAVs. Ukraine’s Lasar’s Group repelled mechanized assaults in Novopavlivka and Dobropillia, destroying enemy tanks. Additionally, Ukraine is launching Mission Control, a unified digital system for drone operations, to enhance battlefield coordination.
